text: I am surrounded by the hum of artificial lights and the calculated geometry of these rooms. There’s a sense of order and purpose, as if each object was placed with intention, yet the emptiness of the space pulls at the edges of my comfort. I feel out of place—an observer more than a participant, searching for meaning amid conference tables and exhibits. The presence of another person, blurred in movement, reminds me of the quiet anxiety that surfaces when I am both seen and unseen. In this environment that showcases research and innovation, I feel introspective: uncertain, a little self-protective, but also curious—aware of the boundaries between myself and the world around me.
